def: s.m. pl. (of ẕaḵẖīra), Treasures, stores, provisions, victuals.

ety: for A. ضخامة, inf. n. of ضخم 'to be or become large,' &c.
def: s.f. Bigness, thickness, bulkiness.
more: n. f. ضخم large. Thickness; volume; bulk.

def: s. f. See ضخامت ẓaḵẖāmat.

ety: for A. ذخيرة ẕaḵẖīrat, v.n. fr. ذخر 'to hoard, treasure,' &c.
def: s.m. A thing hoarded, treasured, or laid up; treasure, hoard; provisions, victuals, stores, munition
subs: v.t. To treasure, store up, lay up.

ety: for A. ذخيرة ẕaḵẖīrat, v.n. fr. ذخر 'to hoard, treasure,' &c.
def: s.m. A thing hoarded, treasured, or laid up; treasure, hoard; provisions, victuals, stores, munition
more: n. m. ذخر stored. Provisions (اتَالا 1, 2,); treasure.
s. m. Treasure, provisions, victuals, stores, munition.
subs: v.t. To treasure, store up, lay up.

ety: intens. n. f. زخر 'to rise' (the sea
def: adj. Full and swelling (the sea), overflowing, raging.
more: adj. (superl. from زخر It abounded) Overflowing, raging (the sea).

ety: prob. jagh˚, fr. Zend rt. jan, redupl.+aff. man; S. जघ, by redupl. fr. rt. हन्+मन्
def: s.m. (f.?), Wound, sore, scar, cut, gash; fracture; injury; damage; loss (syn. ghāʼo; ćoṭ)
more: n. m. 1. A wound; sore; cut; gash ( choṭ , 1.). Bandar kā zakhm . Prov. A monkey's wound. (A wound kept green by constant irritation). 2. Damage; loss. zakhm bharnā , v. n. See bharnā , 12. zakhm par namak chhiṛaknā , v. a. 1. To put salt on one's wound. 2. To afflict the afflicted. zakhm paṛnā , v. n. To become a sore. zakhm paknā , v. n. To suppurate. zakhm-i-talvār , n. m. A sword cut, or wound. zakhm denā , v. a. 1. To give a cut or wound; to stab. 2. To hurt; injure. zakhm-i-kārī , A fatal or mortal wound. zakhm harā h. , v. n. To be renewed (a wound).
s. m. f. A wound, sore, scar, cut. زخم رسا zaḵẖm-rasā, A deep wound. زخم کهانا zaḵẖm khānā, To be wounded. زخم کاري zaḵẖm-i-kārī, A mortal wound.
subs: To sprinkle salt on a wound; to afflict or torture the afflicted
(-ko), To give (one) a wound or cut, &c., to stab; to wound, hurt, injure
or zaḵẖm-kārī, s.m. A mortal wound
A wound to re-open or to be renewed
'A monkey's wound'; a wound kept green by constant irritation
s.m. A wound with the eye, a fascinating look.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَخْم (zaxm).

ety: see zaḵẖm
def: s.m. Plectrum (of a guitar, or lyre, &c.); bow (of a violin, &c.).
more: s. The plectrum of a musical instrument, a small metal instrument for playing on the guitar.

ety: rel. n. fr. zaḵẖm
def: s.m.f. Wounded, hurt; slain, killed by a wound; — a wounded person
more: adj. Wounded; hurt. zakhmī k. , v. a. To wound; hurt. zakhmī h., zakhm khānā , v. n. To be wounded.
n. A wounded man or woman.
subs: v.t. To wound, hurt, to inflict wounds on

ety: v.n. fr. زکو 'to be or become pure'
def: adj. & s.m. Pure, virtuous, good, righteous, pious; continent; one who gives the prescribed portion of his wealth to the poor.
more: (part. from زکا) 1. adj. Pure, pious. 2. Continent. 3. s. m. One who regularly pays the زکاة zakāt, q. v.

ety: rel. n. of ẕakā, q.v.
def: adj. Sharp, acute, intelligent, clever, of penetrating intellect, sagacious (syn. ẕahīn); — fiery, flaming
more: (from ذکا) adj. 1. Acute, ingenious. 2. Fiery, flaming. ذکي المعي ẕakīyu-l-maﻌi, Of a splendid genius.
subs: Of a splendid or brilliant genius.

ety: act. part. n. of ذکر 'to remember,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Remembering; grateful; — rememberer; a grateful person; one who praises God.
more: part. act. (of ذکر) Remembering, grateful, a praiser of God.

ety: v.n. fr. زقم 'to eat quickly,' &c.
def: s.m. An infernal tree (mentioned in the Qorʼān), the fruit of which is supposed to resemble the heads of devils and terrible serpents; — a prickly plant, cactus, Euphorbia antiquorum (?).
more: n. m. A prickly plant; cactus; Euphorbia antiquorum (?). The fruit is supposed to be the heads of devils.
s. A thorny tree so called.

ety: Zend zaurura, fr. zaurva, rt. zar = S. जर्
def: adj. Old, decrepit, grey-headed (man or woman); — name of a famous Persian prince, father of the celebrated Rustam, and son of Sām, son of Narīmān (he was called zāl, or zāl-ě-zar, because he came into the world with white hair, and a red countenance; the former like that of an old man, the latter of golden hue).
more: 1. adj. Old, decrepit, grey-haired (man or woman). 2. n. prop. (or زال زر) The name of the father of Rustam, and son of Sām, son of Narīmān. He was called زال زر ( zāl-i-zar ) because he came into the world with white hair, and a red countenance; the former like that of an old man, the latter of golden hue.

r: ẕāl, dāl-ě-mǒʻjama, th, thou, ze, z., ẕuʼl-ḥijja, abjad
def: called dāl-ě-mǒʻjama, is the thirteenth letter of the Hindūstānī or Urdū alphabet, and ninth of the Arabic (from which it is taken). It is pronounced by the Arabs like th in the English thou; but in Urdū its sound is not to be distinguished from that of ze, which is exactly our own letter z. It is used, by way of abbreviation, for ẕuʼl-ḥijja, the last month of the Arabic lunar year; and in reckoning by abjad, it stands for 700.
more: called dāl-i-mojma , is the ninth letter of the Arabic alphabet. It is pronounced by the Arabs like th in the Eng. word thou ; but in Hindustani its sound is not to be distinguished from that of z . In abjad it stands for 700, and, by way of abbreviation, it is written for ذرالحجه the last month of the Arabic lunar year.

ety: inf. n. fr. زلق 'to slip,' &c.
def: s.m. Onanism, masturbation (syn. maṭholā; hath-ras).
s.m. = ẕull, and ẕillat, q.v. s.m. Baseness, meanness, &c. (see ẕillat, and next).

ety: inf. n. of زلّ 'to slip'
def: s.m. Slipping, stumbling, falling; erring, blundering; a slip, fall; deficiency, loss.
more: s. m. 1. Erring, blundering. 2. Stumbling, falling. 3. Deficiency, loss.

def: s.m. (inf. n. of ضلّ 'to err, go astray,' &c.), = P ضلالت ẓalālat, s.f. (for A. ضلالة inf. n.of ضلّ), Erring, going astray — error, fault, vice; ruin, perdition.
more: s. m. Errour, fault, vice, ruin, perdition.

ety: v.n. fr. ضلّ 'to err,' &c.
def: s.m. Error, &c. ( = ẓalāl, q.v.). s.m. = ẕull, and ẕillat, q.v. s.m. Baseness, meanness, &c. (see ẕillat, and next).

ety: for A. ذلالة, inf. n. of ذلّ 'to be base,' &c.
def: s.f. Baseness, abjectness, contemptibleness; obsequiousness.
s.f. (for A. ضلالة inf. n. of ضلّ), = A ضلال ẓalāl, s.m. (inf. n. of ضلّ 'to err, goastray,' &c.), Erring, going astray — error, fault, vice; ruin, perdition.
more: s. f. Errour, fault, vice, ruin, perdition.

ety: v.n. fr. ظلم; iv اظلم 'to be dark'
def: s.m. Darkness ( = z̤ulmat).
more: s. m. Darkness. ¶ z̤allām (adj. superl.) A great oppressor; very tyrannical.

def: s.m. pl. (of zalzala), Tremblings; earthquakes.

ety: v.n. fr. ذل 'to be base,' &c.
def: adj. Base, mean, vile, wretched, contemptible, despicable, ignominious; brought low, abased, humbled, disgraced, insulted
more: adj. 1. Base; contemptible. 2. Shameless ( be-hayā , 1.). zalīl k .; (Rare) zillat denā , v. a. 1. To insult; hoot at; shame. 2. To disgrace; degrade. 3. To dishonor; defile. zalīl h , v. n. To be insulted, disgraced.
(from ذلّ) adj. Abject, submissive, mean, contemptible, wretched, base. ذليل کرنا ẕalīl karnā, To abase, to debase.
subs: v.t. To abase, debase, disgrace, render abject or vile, to humble, bring low, to insult
v.n. To be brought low, be abased, be disgraced, be insulted.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذلیل (zalīl), from Arabic ذَلِيل (ḏalīl). Compare Punjabi ذليل / ਜ਼ਲੀਲ (zalīl).

ety: v.n. fr. ظلّ 'to shade'
def: adj. Having shade, shady, affording shelter.

ety: act. part. of ظلم 'to act wrongfully or tyrannically'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Acting wrongfully, tyrannical, unjust, cruel; — a tyrant, an oppressor, a wrong-doer, a cruel person; (met.) an unrelenting sweetheart, a cruel mistress
more: n. m. ظلم did wrong. 1. A tyrant ( sitamgār ). 1. Zālim kī umar kotāh . Prov. The tyrant's term of life is short. 2. Jo ki zālim hai woh hargiz phūltā phaltā nahīṅ, Sabz hote khet dekhā hai kahīṅ shamshīr kā? Nāsikh. No tyrant yet a golden harvest e'er could own, Who ever saw the waving corn by sword yet grown? 3. Darvāzah maë-kade kā na kar band, mohtasib; Zālim, Khudā se ḍar! ki dar-i-taubā bāz hai . Zauq. The door of taverns free thou public censor do not bar! Tyrant! fear God who leaves the door of penitence ajar. 4. Zālim kā zor sir par . Prov. The tyrant smites on the head. 2. ( Poet .) A cruel mistress. Nainā tere zālim zor, manhiyār kī! Song. Your eyes possess a cruel force, oh bangle-seller fair! zāl'im ; Illit. zālam , adj. 1. See (انيائي 3). 2. Cruel; barbarous; wanton. Zālam jaṭṭī ne toṛe haiṅ ber, dhele ke pāo ser, jhāṛī būṭī ke ber! Delhī Street cry. Plums plucked by a crue…
(part. act. of ظلم) 1. s. m. A tyrant, an oppressor. 2. adj. Tyrannical, unjust. 3. ( met. ) Applied to a beloved person or mistress. ظالم گداز z̤ālim-gudāz, A destroyer of the tyrannical.
subs: s.m. A destroyer of tyrants or oppressors (applied to a ruler who maintains justice and good order).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic ظَالِم (ẓālim).

ety: z̤ālim, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Wrong-doing, tyranny, oppression ( = z̤ulm).

ety: v.n. fr. ذلق 'to be sharp or eloquent'
def: s.m. A sharp and ready or eloquent tongue; readiness of speech, volubility
more: (Heb. ) s. m. A prompt and ready or eloquent tongue. حرف الذلق ḥarfu-ẕ-ẕalḳ, Lingual letter; as ل رن.

ety: inf. n. of ضلّ 'to err,' &c.
def: s.m. Erring, going astray, &c. ( = ẓalāl, q.v.). s.m. = ẕull, and ẕillat, q.v. s.m. Baseness, meanness, &c. (see ẕillat, and next).
more: s. m. An errour, a vice.

ety: act. part. of ضلّ 'to err, go astray,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Erring, straying, going astray; becoming lost; — forgetting; — a prevaricator, &c.
more: part. act. (of ضلّ) 1. Straying. 2. s. m. A prevaricator.

ety: for A. زلة, v.n. fr. زلّ 'to slip,' &c.
def: s.m. Food carried home by the guests from an entertainment.
more: s. m. Victuals carried home by the guests from an entertainment.

ety: intens. n. fr. ضلّ; see ẓall
def: adj. Going greatly astray, erring extremely.

ety: v.n. intens. fr. ظلم 'to act wrongfully,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Very unjust or tyrannical; — a great tyrant or oppressor.

ety: = S. जालुका
def: s.f. A leech.

ety: v.n. fr. ذلّ 'to be easy,' &c.
def: adj. Gentle, tractable, docile, submissive, obedient.

ety: intens. adj. fr. ظلم; see z̤ulm
def: adj. & s.m. Acting wrongfully, most oppressive, tyrannical, cruel; — one who acts wrongfully, &c.; a grievous wrong-doer.
more: adj. (superl. of ظلم) Cruel, most oppressive, tyrannical.

ety: for A. زلزلة, inf. n. of زلزل 'to put in a state of motion'
def: s.m. Violent motion, commotion, convulsion; earthquake.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَلْزَلَه (zalzala), from Arabic زَلْزَلَة (zalzala). First attested in c. 1739 as Middle Hindi زلزلہ (zlzlh /⁠zalzala⁠/).

def: s.m. pl. (of ẕamīma), Crimes, misdemeanours; reprehensible or base qualities.
s. plur. of ضمير ẓamīr, q. v.
s.f. pl. (of ẓamīr, q.v.), Personal pronouns, &c.
s.m. Time ( = zamān, q.v.). s.m. Time, period, duration; season; a long time; an age; (in Gram.) tense; — the world; the heavens; fortune, destiny
subs: To see the world, to gain experience
s.m. A time-server, temporizer, turn-coat, sycophant
s.f. Time-serving, turning with the tide, yielding to fortune; sycophancy; hypocrisy; necessity; — zamāna-sāzī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize, &c.
According to the times, according to the fashion of the day
adj. Well-timed; favoured by the times or by fortune, fortunate.

ety: inf. n. of ضمن 'to be or become responsible,' &c.
def: s.m. Suretiship, guaranteeship, security (see next).

ety: v.n. fr. iv of زمن
def: s.m. Time, period, duration; season; a long time; an age; (in Gram.) tense; — the world; the heavens; fortune, destiny
more: (Heb. from زمن Heb. To define, to determine, to appoint) s. m. 1. Time, an age. 2. The world. 3. Fortune. 4. The heavens. 5. Tense. (Plur. ازمنه). زمانه ساز zamāna-sāz, Turncoat, time-server, temporizer. زمانه سازي zamāna-sāz-ī, Turning with the tide, timeserving, yielding to fortune. زمانه سازي کرنا zamāna-sāz-ī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize. زمانه موافق zamāna-muwāfiḳ, Fortunate.
subs: To see the world, to gain experience
s.m. A time-server, temporizer, turn-coat, sycophant
s.f. Time-serving, turning with the tide, yielding to fortune; sycophancy; hypocrisy; necessity; — zamāna-sāzī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize, &c.
According to the times, according to the fashion of the day
adj. Well-timed; favoured by the times or by fortune, fortunate.

ety: for A. زمانة, from zamān
def: s.m. Time, period, duration; season; a long time; an age; (in Gram.) tense; — the world; the heavens; fortune, destiny
more: n. m. Z. damān ; A. زمن stayed a long time. 1. Time; period; duration. 2. An age (H. jug ). Āj kal achchhe kā zamāna nahīṅ hai . 3. The world. Zamāne meṅ to tum sarīkā hogā nahīṅ! No one like you in the world! 4. ( Gram .) Tense or time (H. kāl ). zamāna dekhnā , v. a. To see the world; to get experience. zamāna-sāz , n. m. A time-server (خوشامدي). zamāna-sāzī , n. f. Time-serving; sycophancy. zamāne ke muāfiq . According to the fashion of the day. zamāna muāfiq h . To be fortunate.

(Heb. from زمن Heb. To define, to determine, to appoint) s. m. 1. Time, an age. 2. The world. 3. Fortune. 4. The heavens. 5. Tense. (Plur. ازمنه). زمانه ساز zamāna-sāz, Turncoat, time-server, temporizer. زمانه سازي zamāna-sāz-ī, Turning with the tide, timeserving, yielding to fortune. زمانه سازي کرنا zamāna-sāz-ī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize. زمانه موافق zamāna-muwāfiḳ, Fortunate.
subs: To see the world, to gain experience
s.m. A time-server, temporizer, turn-coat, sycophant
s.f. Time-serving, turning with the tide, yielding to fortune; sycophancy; hypocrisy; necessity; — zamāna-sāzī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize, &c.
According to the times, according to the fashion of the day
adj. Well-timed; favoured by the times or by fortune, fortunate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَمَانَه (zamāna). Equivalent to زَمان (zamān) + ـہ (-h). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi زمانہ (zmanh).

ety: for A. ضمانة, fr. ẓamān
def: s.f. Security, &c. ( = ẓāminī, q.v.)
more: n. f. Security (ضامني). zamānat-i-hifz-i-aman, yā adam-fasād , Security to keep the peace. zamānat dākhil k., yā denā . To furnish security; give bail. zamānat-i-kāfī . Sufficient security. zamānat ke qābil . Bailable. zamānat le-kar chhoṛnā, zamānat pe rihā k. , v. a. To discharge on bail; to hold to bail. zamānat-nāmah , n. m. Bail bond; security bond; a deed of suretyship. zamānat-i-nek-chalanī , n. f. Security for good behaviour. gair qābil-ul-zamānat . Not bailable.
subs: v.t. To discharge on bail, to hold to bail
or ẓamānat denā, To furnish security, give bail
s.m. Bail-bond, security-bond, deed of suretiship.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضمانت (zamānat), from Arabic ضَمَانَة (ḍamāna) / ضَمان (ḍamān).

ety: for A. ضمانةً, acc. of ẓamānat
def: adv. By way of security.
more: n. m. One who gives bail or security.

ety: rel. n. fr. ẓamānat
def: s.m. One who gives bail or security (syn. ẓāmin, and ẓamānat dene-wālā).

ety: v.n. fr. ذنب 'to follow the tail, or the track of'
def: s.m. Sin, crime, fault, misdemeanour, misdeed.
more: s. m. A crime, sin, fault. (Plur. ذنوب ẕunūb ). ¶ ẕanab (Heb. ) s. m. 1. A tail. 2. The descending node.

def: s.m. The white jasmine, Jasminum sambac; the iris, Iris germanica, or I. sambac; the lily.
more: s. Name of a flower, the white jasmine, a lily, or an iris.

ety: A. zabīl, zambīl, zimbīl
def: s.f. A basket (made of palm-leaves); a dried and hollow gourd (for keeping things in); a purse, bag, wallet.
more: n. f. A bag; wallet. Amar aiyār kī zambīl . The horn of plenty.
s. f. 1. A basket. 2. A purse, a wallet. (Adopted in Arab. plur. زنابيل).

ety: prob. corr. of زنبور, q.v.
def: s.m. (local), A vice, nippers, forceps.
more: s. m. A vice, nippers, forceps.

ety: for A. zumbur
def: s.m. A hornet; bee, wasp; a vice, &c. (see زمبور zambūr); — a camel-swivel ( = zambūrak).
more: n. m. 1. A wasp. 2. Bell-mouthed guns carried on camels. 3. Pincers; dental forceps.
(Chald. Heb. from To lead, to speak; so named, perhaps, from following its leader; or, from its humming noise) s. m. f. 1. A hornet. 2. A bee. (Plur. زنابير). See, also, H. زمبور zambūr.

def: s.m. A hornet; a large bee; — a camel-swivel; — a kind of guitar made of two gourds (syn. kingrī); the point of this instrument.
more: s. m. 1. A hornet, large bee. 2. The point of a musical stringed instrument called in Hindī کنگري. 3. A small gun. زنبورچي zambūr-chī, A fusileer.

ety: zambūr+dim. aff. ak = S. अक
def: s.f. A small gun or cannon, a camel-swivel ( = zambūr, and zambūra).
more: s. f. A small gun or swivel, usually carried on a camel.

def: s.m. A fusileer.
s.m. Intense cold; cold.
more: (from زمـہر The eye being red with anger) s. m. Cold, intense cold.

ety: from the preceding
def: adj. Intensely cold.

ety: Pārsī zamī; Pehl. damīk; Zend zem; S. जम्
def: s.f. The earth, &c. ( = zamīn), q.v.
subs: s.m. = zamīn-dār; and zamī-dārī, s.f. = zamīn-dārī; qq.v.s.v. zamīn.

def: corrupt. of زميندار zamīn-dār, q. v. Landlord, &c.

ety: for A. ضميمة, n. of un. fr. ضميم, v.n. fr. ضمّ 'to collect, to contract'
def: s.m. An adjunct, addition, augmentation; a thing incorporated (in another); an appendix (syn. tatimma), a supplement.
more: n. m. 1. An appendix ( tatimmā ). 2. An extra, a supplement to a newspaper.
s. m. Addition, thing incorporated, incorporation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَمِیمَه (zamīma).

ety: for A. ذميمة ẕamīmat, fr. ẕamīm, 'blamed'; rt. ذمّ 'to blame'
def: s.m. An act, or a quality, to be blamed or reprehended; a reprehensible act, bad quality, vice, misdeed, crime, misdemeanour.

ety: Zend zemaênya, fr. zem; see zamī
def: s.f. The earth; soil, land, ground; floor; foundation, ground-work; the ground of a picture; region, country
more: n. f. Z. zamīk , Vedic S. jam . 1. See بهوم 1. Unkā kyā ṭhikānā hai, kabhī zamīn meṅ, kabhī āsmān meṅ . There's no holding him, his feet now on the earth, now in the skies. 2. Zamīn meṅ lāt māre to pānī nikal paṛe ! Wom. He is so strong that a kick from his foot would. make water issue from the earth! 2. The ground of a picture. 3. Metre. Zamīn-i-sher. zamīn āsmān kā farq . A great difference. zamīn āsmān ke gullābe milānā , v. a. lit . to make the sky and earth meet. To exaggerate; tell great lies. zamīṅ-bos h. , v. n. To kiss the ground; prostrate oneself. zamīn par se kuchh paṛā pānā . See āsmān se girnā. zamīn pakaṛnā , v. n. To stick to a place.

s. f. The earth, ground, soil, land, floor, a region, country, the ground of a picture. زمين کاهونا zamīn kā honā (dakh. i. q. لاچارهونا) To be helpless or destitute (?). زمين بوس zamīn-bos honā, To make a profound bow. زمين پرسي کچهہ پڙاپانا zamīn par se kuchh paṛā pānā, To be overjoyed at finding unexpectedly the object of oneʼs wishes. زمين پکڙني zamīn pakaṛnī, To persevere obstinately in any design, to insist. زمين گير zamīn-gīr, adj. Oppressed or broken down by age or time, paralytick. زمين مين گڙجانا zamīn meṅ gaṛ jānā, To be greatly ashamed.
subs: 'As wide asunder as heaven and earth'; a very great difference
v.n. To kiss the ground, to prostrate oneself; to make a profound bow, to make obeisance
To obtain a thing without exertion or labour; to find or obtain a thing unexpectedly; to be overjoyed at finding unexpectedly the object of one's wishes
To be rooted to a spot; to stick to a place; to persevere obstinately in a design, to stick (to a thing), to insist
adj. Level with the ground; — subterranean
s.m. A kind of sweet potato
v.n. 'To become a patch or piece of the earth,' to mingle with the dust, to die
s.f. 'A neck of land,' an isthmus (syn. ḵẖāk-nāʼe; ḍamru-madh)

adj. & s.m. Falling to the ground; taking to the ground; hanging down to the ground, bent low; bowed; oppressed or broken down by age or time; paralytic; — one who is bowed or bent double, a paralytic
'To fit, or to be put, into the ground,' to be buried
lit. 'To be buried in the earth'; to sink into the earth with shame, to be greatly ashamed
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَمِین (zamīn). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi زمین (zmyn /⁠zamīn⁠/). Compare Punjabi زمین (zamīn), Sindhi زَمِينُ (zamīnu).

ety: act. part. of ضمن 'to be or become responsible for,' &c.
def: s.m. One who is responsible or accountable (for), a surety, guarantee; security, sponsor, bail, bondsman; — a small piece of wood connecting the two tubes of a ḥuqqa
more: n. m. ضمن was responsible. 1. A surety. Girah kā dije, zāmin na hūje . Prov. Your money you may hand, but surety never stand! 2. A small piece of wood connecting the two tubes of a huqqa. 3. (Ped. for H. jāman , q. v.) Rennet. zāmin dar zāmin . A collateral security. zāmin kī barīat . Discharge of a surety. zāmin h. , n. m. To be surety for another; to give or stand bail; to guarantee; engage for another. zāmin denā , v. a. To give a surety. fel-zāmin . A surety or security for good conduct or for a person's refraining from any proscribed offence or misdemeanor. māl-zāmin . A security for the discharge of a debt; recognizances; bail.
(part. act. of ضمن) s. m. A surety, a sponsor, security, bail, bondsman.
subs: s.m. One having a bail or surety, person bailed
(-kā), To be surety (for), to give or stand bail (for); to guarantee; to engage (for)
s.m. A surety or security for good conduct, or for (a person's) refraining from any prescribed offence or misdemeanour
s.m. A security for the discharge of a debt; recognizances; bail.

ety: zamīn+dār, qq.v.
def: s.m. Land-holder, land-owner, landlord, landed proprietor; farmer.
more: n. m. A landholder; landlord; landed proprietor; landowner; lord of a manor. Zimīndār ko kisān, bachche ko masān ! Prov. The tenant to a landlord, merasmus to a child! zamīṅ-dārah , n. 1. The allowance to a zamīn-dār who is set aside. 2. Country ( dihāt ). zamīṅ-dār aur jotā . Landlord and tenant. zamīṅ-dār'nī, zamīn-dāran , n. f. The wife of a zamīn-dār .
(زمين Ground, and part. act. of داشتن To have, to hold) A landlord, a landholder, a farmer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زمیندار (zamīn-dār).

ety: or zamīndār-āna
def: s.m. Allowance made to a zamīndār who is set aside; — country (syn. dihāt).

ety: zamīndār+S. aff. इनी
def: s.f. The wife of a zamīndār.
s.f. Landed estate, landed property, estate, tenure in severalty, fee-farm, freehold, fief; land or estate held by a zamīndār; the office or tenure of a zamīndār
more: n. f. 1. Real or landed estate; landed property; estate; tenure in severalty; fee farm; freehold; manor; fief. 2. The office or tenure of a zamīn-dār ; the land or estates held by him. zamīṅ-dārī paṭṭā , n. m. A document granted by Government in recognition of the possessions of a zamīn-dār. zamīṅ-dārī jama , n. f. The amount of revenue which the holder of a zamīn-dārī has engaged to pay to the Government. zamīn-doz , adj. 1. Level with the ground. 2. Subterranean ( bhūm tale ). zamī dekhnā , v. a. Wom. To vomit ( qai k .). zamīn-i-gair-mazrūā , n. f. Uncaltivated land. zamīn kā paivand h. , Wom. v. n. To mingle with the dust; to die. zamīn kā karāyā mahsūl . Revenue from land; land rent. zamīn kā gaz , A great traveller. zamīn kā mālik , n. m. A landowner. zamīn khodnā , v. a. To dig; excavate. zamīn kī bhej . Ground rent. zamīn kī gardan ; P. khāknāë ; S. डमरु मध्य ( Geog .) Isthmus. z…
s. f. A landed estate, a fief.
subs: s.m. A document granted by Government in recognition of the estate of a zamīndār
s.f. The amount of revenue which the holder of a zamīndārī has engaged to pay to the Government.

ety: zamīndār+S. aff. इनी
def: s.f. The wife of a zamīndār.
more: s. f. The wife of a landholder or farmer.

def: adj. Of or relating to the earth, earthly, earthy, terrestrial.
adj. Of or relating to the earth; earthly; terrestrial.

ety: ẓāmin, q.v. + ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Guarantee, security, surety, bail; pledge
more: n. f. Security; bail; pledge; guarantee. zāmnī par chhoṛnā, zāmnī manzūr k. , v. a. To admit to, or accept bail. zar-i-zāminī , n. m. Money paid as a security. zanjīr-zāminī , n. f. Chain security. A number of persons binding themselves severally or jointly for each other; the joint responsibility of the cultivators of a village or district for the whole revenue or for money borrowed of a banker. fel-zāminī , n. f. Security to keep the peace or for good conduct. māl-zāminī , n. f. Surety for the payment of any pecuniary obligation.
s. f. Surety, bail. ضامني قبول کرنا ẓāmin-ī ḳabūl karnā, To admit as bail.
subs: (-ko), To release on bail, to admit to bail
or ẓāminī manz̤ūr karnā (-kī), To accept the security or bail (of), to admit as bail

s.f. 'Chain-security,' a number of persons binding themselves severally or jointly for one another; joint responsibility (as of the cultivators of a village or district for the whole revenue thereof, or for money borrowed, &c.)
s.f. Security for good conduct, or to keep the peace, &c.
s.f. Surety for the payment of any pecuniary obligation.

ety: v.n. fr. اضمر 'to suppress,' &c., iv of ضمر 'to be lean'
def: s.f. A personal pronoun; — s.m. and f. Heart, mind; the recesses of the mind, the secret thoughts; thought, reflection, sense, conscience, conception, idea, comprehension.
more: n. f. 1. ( Ped .) The mind; heart. 2. ( Gram .) A pronoun.
s. f. 1. The mind, heart, thought, reflection, sense, conscience, conception, idea, comprehension. 2. A personal pronoun.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَمِیر (zamīr), from Arabic ضَمِير (ḍamīr). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi ضمیر (zamīr).

ety: rel. n. fr. ẓamīr
def: adj. Concealed; of or relating to the mind or thought; pronominal.
more: adj. Of the mind or thought, pronominal.

def: adj. Of or belonging to winter; wintry.

ety: inf. n. of ذمّ 'to blame'
def: s.f. Blame, censure, reproach, accusation, disparagement, detraction ( = maẕammat, which is the more common form).
more: s. f. Blame, reproach, accusation, detraction.

ety: inf. n. of ضمّ 'to draw together, to contract,' &c.
def: s.m. Contraction; conjunction; incorporation; — the vowel-sound u (pronounced like the English u in push; — cf. ẓamma).
more: n. m. The vowel point (ُ) pronounced as the Italian short u or as the English u in full ( pesh , 2).
(Chald. To contract, to draw together) s. m. 1. Contraction, conjunction, incorporation. 2. The vowel point (ُ) pronounced as the Italian short u or as the English u in full.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضم (zamm), from Arabic ضَمّ (ḍamm).

ety: for A. ضمة, inf. n. of un. fr. ضمّ; see ẓamm
def: s.m. The sign () of the vowel-sound u (termed ẓamm; cf. pesh).

ety: v.n. fr. رزمزم 'to make a confused sound'
def: s.m. Name of a celebrated well at Mecca, called Hagar's well.
more: s. m. The name of a well at Mecca, called also Hagarʼs well.

ety: for A. زمزمة, inf. n. of زمزم; see zamzam
def: s.m. Singing, chanting, intoning; chant; modulation; hum, a low murmuring sound
more: n. m. زم a distant sound. Singing; chanting; modulation of the voice. zam zam is the refrain of some English and Italian songs. zamzama-pardāz , n. m. A singer; songster.
(quadril. inf. i. of زمزم) s. m. Singing, modulation, a concert. زمزمه پرداز zamzama-pardāz, Singer, songster.

def: s.f. A goblet or vessel filled with water from the well Zamzam, q.v.
more: n. f. A goblet filled with water from the zamzam well at Mecca.

ety: Pehl. zan; Zend ghena; S. ग्ना, for ज्ञा
def: s.f. Woman; wife
more: ( In comp .) Striking; as rah-zan a highwayman.
n. f. A woman; a wife (استري). zan-murīd , adj. Henpecked ( jorū kā mazūr ). zan-i-mankūhah . A married woman (Opp. of mad/?/khālā , a concubine). be-zan o farzand . Without wife and children.

1. s. f. A woman, a wife. زن کرنا يا لينا zan-karnā or -lenā, To marry. زن مريد zan-murīd, adj. Hen-pecked. 2. part. act. (from زدن To beat, to strike) in compos. Beating, striking, beater, striker; as, رهزن rah-zan, A highwayman.
subs: or zan lenā, To take a wife, to marry
s.f. A married woman, a lawful wife (as opp. to zan-ě-madḵẖūla, 'concubine')
wety: PIE word *gʷḗn Borrowed from Classical Persian زن (zan, “woman; wife”). Cognate with English queen.

wety: PIE word *gʷḗn Borrowed from Classical Persian زن (zan, “woman; wife”). Cognate with English queen.

ety: of zan
def: s.m. Men who affect the speech and ways of women.
more: part. pres. (from زدن) Striking, beating, striking up.

ety: zan+āna = Zend ana, or āna = S. अन; or zanān+a = Zend a = S. a
def: adj. & s.m. Female, feminine; womanly; effeminate; — an effeminate person, a woman; a eunuch (an individual of a class whose occupation is singing and dancing, &c.); — women's apartments, seraglio, harem; — women, wives.
more: adj. 1. Female; feminine. 2. Womanish; effeminate. 3. Weak; imbecile; impotent. Mue zanāne merī qadr, ūhī! kyā jāneṅ? Mere to āne kī haiṅ mard ārzū karte . Wom. Jān Sāhab. Pish! what knows he of my rare worth? the wretched impotent! A man would burn to feel my passion's liquid fire bespent. zanāna madarsah . Ped. A girl's school. zanāna-makān, zanān-khānah . The female apartments. zanān'a , n. m. 1. A woman; a female. Hai zanāna, bhes mardāna? Prov. Her sex female, dressed as a male! 2. A family; a wife. Savārī kī savārī, zanānā sāth! (Fac.) Said to one who rides a mare. An animal to ride, a wife beside. 3. The female apartments. 4. A eunuch who is also a catamite ( hījṛā ). zanāne , n. m. A class of eunuchs whose occupation is singing and dancing and sodomy.
1. adj. Female, feminine, effeminate, womanly. 2. s. m. Female apartments, a seraglio.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنانه. By surface analysis, انہ + زن

ety: zanān, pl. of zan, q.v.+ī = S. इन्

more: adj. Belonging to women; feminine. zanānī bolī ; Poet. r/?/khtī , n. f. Women's language.
adj. Of the women, of the seraglio.

def: n. m. Men who affect the speech and style of women.

s. m. 1. Rust. 2. A small bell. 3. The country of Zang-bār in Africa. زنگ لگنا zang lagnā, To become rusty. زنگ آلود zang-ālūd, adj. Rusty, covered with rust.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنگ (zang).

more: s. Black ink: name of a country.

more: n. m. An Ethiop; a negro; Moor.

s. f. Dry ginger. (Sans. शृंगवेर comp. of शृंग A horn, and वेर A body; q. d. Shaped like a horn).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنجبیل (zanjabīl), from Arabic زَنْجَبِيل (zanjabīl).

def: s.m. A necklace; — chain-stitch.

def: s.f. A little woman.

more: n. m. 1. A eunuch. 2. A dancing boy. zankhā , adj. 1. Effeminate. 2. Weak in the loins.

more: n. m. ظن opined. 1. Impression; notion. 2. Conjecture ( bharam ). zann-i-gālib . Strong or violent presumption; great probability. zann-i-fāsid, gumān-i-bad . 1. An evil thought. 2. Suspicion. bad zan , adj. Suspicions; sceptical.

def: s.m. = sannāṭā, q.v. s.m. Loud or violent sound, rumbling noise, clatter (made by wind and rain or hail, &c. at a distance), howling (of the wind), roaring, roar (of waves, or a conflagration, &c.); violent blast or gust; a dashing or driving (of rain, &c.); ringing, whizzing, whiz (of bullets, &c.); vehemence, animation, briskness and eagerness; — a transport of passion or rage; — a howling wilderness, a dreary place or spot; a stunning blow or shock; a state of alarm or terror, consternation; amazement; anything monstrous or frightful; — adj. Enormous, huge, vast, monstrous; — still, silent, lonely, dreary, desolate, frightful
subs: (-ko), To receive a shock, to be shocked, be amazed, be terror-stricken, be stunned, to fall into a swoon
adj. (f. -ī), Violent, terrible, awful, fearful, monstrous, appalling, shocking; dreary, desolate
v.n. To fall into or continue in a state of alarm or terror, to be stricken motionless with terror or astonishment
v.n. To be or become motionless (with astonishment or fear, &c.), to be paralyzed (with fear, &c.).

def: n. m. The whiz of a solid body through the air.
(rel. n. fr. z̤ann), Thought, supposed; suspected.

(s. जानु ) s. m. The knee, the lap.
subs: v.n. To change the knees, to rest the knees alternately (in kneeling)
To sit on the hams, to kneel.

def: s. (dakh. i. q. آوازپر) Sound of a wing (?).

ety: fr. Zend zar = S. हृ (हृणीयते; and, in some senses, app. = S. हृ, हर्); cf. āzār
def: s.m. Groan, plaint, lamentation, wailing (cf. zārī); desire, wish; collection, multitude, crowd; (as a suffix) place where anything grows in abundance, place, bed, garden ( = stān or istān; e.g. gul-zār, 'a rose-garden') — adj. Groaning, lamenting, afflicted; thin, lean, weak
more: ( In comp .) Place; multitude, as gulzār a garden of roses, sabzah-zār a meadow. zār zār, zār nizār, zār qatār; Illit. zār be- zār . Great lamentation. zār zār ronā , v. n. To weep bitterly. Gale lag-ke rone lagī zār zār; Kīyā apne tan man ko us par nisār . Mīr Hasan. zār o nizār , adj. 1. Emaciated; lean. 2. Weak.

1. s. m. Desire, wish. 2. A groan, lamentation. 3. adj. Afflicted, groaning, lamenting. زار زار zār-zār, Great lamentation. زارنالي zār-nālī, Lamentation. زارو نزار zār-o-nizār, Emaciated. 4. (in compos.) It implies place, multitude; as, گلزار gul-zār, s. m. A garden of roses.
subs: and zār-qitār, s.m. Great lamentation; — adv. With great lamentation; piteously, bitterly, exceedingly, to excess (e.g. zār-zār ronā, 'to weep bitterly')
s.m. Great lamentation; — adj. Thin, lean, emaciated; weak, feeble.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زار (zār), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *jāráyati.

ety: Zend zairi, rt. zar = S. हरि 'yellow'; but cf. Zend zaranya, 'gold'; and S. हिरण्य्
def: s.m. Gold; money, riches, wealth
more: n. m. 1. Gold. 2. Riches ( drab ). Zar hai to nar hai, nahīṅ panchhī be-par hai . Prov. If you 've wealth you are a man; if not, a wingless bird. zar-i-asl , n. m. A principal sum bearing interest. zar-i-amānat , n. m. Deposit money; trust fund. zar-i-āmdanī , n. m. Proceeds; receipts; income; profits; dividend; revenue. zar-i-bāqī, bāqī , n. m. Outstanding balance. zar-baft , n. m. Brocade; cloth of gold. zar-i-byā'na ; H. sāī , n. m. Earnest money. zar-parast , adj. 1. Venal; mercenary. 2. Selfish; sordid. zar-i-peshgī , n. m. Payment in advance. zar-i-tāvān ; H. ḍanḍ , n. m. Penalty; fine. zar-i-tahsīl , n. m. Collections; revenue. zar-i-jāfarī , n. m. Gold of the purest kind. zar-i-jurmānā . A fine. zar-i-kharchā . Costs of suit. zar-i-kharīd , Purchase money. zar-kharīd , adj. Purchased with one's own money. zar-khez, zar-rez , adj. lit . producing gold. Fertile (soil); rich (land).…
n. f. Nonsense; gibberish. ziṛ'ṛī , n. m. A talkative person ( bakvāsī ). ziṛ hāṅknā , v. a. To talk nonsense; chatter.

s. m. Gold, riches, wealth, money. زراندود zar-andūd or زراندوده zar-andūda, Gilt, plated with gold. زرپرست zar-parast, Venal, selfish. زرخريد zar-ḵẖarīd or زرخريده zar-ḵẖarīda, adj. Purchased (as, a slave). زرخيز zar-ḵẖez, adj. Rich (a country), producing gold and silver; fertile. زردار zar-dār, adj. Wealthy. زردوز zar-doz (comp. of زر and part. of دوختن To sew) 1. adj. Embroidered. 2. s. Embroiderer. زردوزي zar-doz-ī, s. f. Embroidery. زردوست zar-dost, adj. Fond of money, covetous. زرسرخ zar-i-surḵẖ, s. m. 1. Gold. 2. Pure gold of a red colour, gold coin. 3. Name of a suit in cards: see under تاج tāj. زرسفيد zar-i-sufaid, s. m. 1. Silver. 2. A suit in cards: see under تاج tāj. زرگر zar-gar, s. m. A goldsmith. زرگري zar-gar-ī, s. f. 1. The business of a goldsmith. 2. A kind of dialect. زرگل zar-i-gul, The yellow stamina of a rose. زرنقد zar-i-naḳd, Ready money, cash. زرنگار zar-nigār, a…
subs: s.m. A principal sum; pure gold
s.m. Income, revenue; receipts, proceeds, profits
part. adj. Covered or plated with gold, gilded, gilt
s.m. Brocade, gold tissue, cloth of gold ( = zar-bāft); — one who works in gold thread, a gold-lace worker
adj. Woven in gold, brocaded, embroidered
s.m. Remaining money, balance, outstanding balance
adj. & s.m. Venal, mercenary; selfish, sordid; — a mammonist; a sordid or selfish person
adj. Purchased with one's own money, purchased (as a slave)
adj. Producing gold; rich (a country); fertile
v.t. To enrich; to make productive, to fertilize
adj. & s.m. Wealthy, rich; — a moneyed man
s.f. Embroidery; — zar-dost, adj. & s.m. Fond of money, covetous; — one who is fond of money, a miser
s.m. 'Red gold,' pure gold; gold; gold coin; — name of a suit in cards (see s.v. tāj)
s.m. Silver; — a suit in cards (see tāj)
s.m. Security for money-payment, a deposit-security
s.m. Amount of a debt; debts
s.m. 'Gold shoe'; embroidered shoe; (met.) a douceur, a bribe
s.f. Beating gold into thin leaves; the business of a gold-beater

s.f. The business of a goldsmith; — a secret or enigmatical mode of speech (similar to what, in English, is called the p language, the letter z being used instead of p; as tuzum kazhāṅ jazātize huzo, i.e. tum kahāṅ jāte ho)
s.m. The yellow stamina of a rose
adj. Gilded, gilt; inlaid with gold
s.m. Money to be got in, balance to be received, debts due, claims, demands, outstandings.

ety: inf. n. of زرع 'to sow'
def: s.m. = zirāʻat, q.v. s.f. Sowing, tilling; tillage, husbandry, agriculture; a sown or cultivated field, sown land; a standing crop
more: s. m. Sowing, seed sown, a sown field.
subs: s.m. One whose business or occupation is sowing or tilling, husbandman, agriculturist
(-kī), To till, to cultivate; to grow crops; to reclaim (land).

def: = P زراتشت zarātusht, prop. n. = زردشت zardusht, q.v.

ety: for A. ظرافة, inf. n. of ظرف 'to be clever, or ingenious, or polite,' &c.
def: s.f. Beauty; ingenuity, &c. ( = z̤arf, q.v.); wit, humour, jocularity, facetiousness, pleasantry, fun, jest.
more: n. f. ظرف was ingenious. Wit; humour; facetiousness. zarāfatan, mazāqan ; H. haṅsī se , adj. Facetiously.
s. f. 1. Beauty. 2. Politeness, elegance, gallantry. 3. Wit, humour, jocularity, facetiousness.

ety: for A. ظرافةً, acc. of z̤arāfat
def: adv. Facetiously, in fun, by way of jest.

ety: v.n. fr. ضرّ 'to harm,' &c.
def: s.m. Harm, injury, hurt, mischief, damage; defect, deficiency, detriment, loss; — poverty; affliction, distress, anguish
more: n. m. ضر hurt. 1. Injury; detriment; wrong ( choṭ , 1). 2. Damage ( zyān ). zarar pahuṅchānā , v. a. To injure; hurt. zarar-i-jismānī , G. G. n. m. Bodily hurt or injury. zarar-i-khafīf , n. m. Slight harm. zarar-rasānī , n. f. Causing injury, annoyance. zarar-i-shadīd , G. G. n. m. Grievous injury. zarar kā khatrah , n. m. Risk of harm. bil-irādah zarar pahuṅchānā , G. G. Voluntarily causing hurt.
s. m. 1. Injury, damage, detriment, loss, ruin. 2. Affliction, anguish.
subs: (-ko), To cause harm to come (to), to harm, hurt, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضرر (zarar), from Arabic ضَرَر (ḍarar).

def: = P زرادشت zarādusht, prop. n. = زردشت zardusht, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of ضرب 'to beat, strike,' &c.
def: s.f. Beating, striking; — stamping; sealing; coining (money); a beating, blow, stroke (of a whip, &c.), wound (of a sword); a sprain, hurt, injury; loss, damage; — stamp, impression (on a coin, &c.); violence, vehemence, emphasis; (in Arith.) multiplying, multiplication; — making or constituting (a saying) proverbial; — a piece (as of cannon, &c.; e.g. ćahār ẓarb top, 'four pieces of cannon'; cf. rās, and zanjīr); — adj. & s.f. Beaten, struck; stamped, coined, &c.; — coined money, a coin
more: n. f. 1. A stroke; blow. 2. Stamp; impression; stamping (coin). 3. ( Math .) Multiplication. 4. Pieces of cannon. Chahār zarab top. zarab uṭhānā , v. n. To suffer loss or damage. zarb-i-shadīd , Grievous hurt. zarab-ul-masal ; H. kahāvat , n. f. A saying; common saying; saw; proverb. zarab-ul masal , adj. Notorious; proverbial; current ( zabāṅ-zad ). zarab-i-khafīf . G. G. A slight wound or blow. zarab denā, yā k .; H. gunā k. , v. a. ( Arith .) To multiply. zarab-i-shamshīr , n. f. A sword-wound or cut. zarab lagānā , v. a. 1. To give a blow; to strike; hammer. 2. To impress upon the mind. zarab murakkab ; H. milā gun , n. f. Compound multiplication. zarab-mufrad ; H. sādhāran-gun , n. f. Simple multiplication. zarab-i-mohlik , n. f. A mortal wound. āla-i-zarab-i-sikka . A coining instrument. hāsil-zarab ; H. phal , n. m. ( Arith .) The product. dār-ul-zarab ; H. ṭaksāl , n. m. The mint…

s. f. 1. A blow, beating. 2. Violence. 3. Multiplication (in arithmetick). 4. Striking, emphasis. 5. Stamping, coining (money), struck, coined. ضرب دينا ẓarb denā, To coin. ضرب المثل ẓarbu-l-mas̤al or ضرب مثل ẓarb-i-mas̤al, s. f. A proverb. دارالضرب dāru-ẓ-ẓarb, s. f. The mint-office.
subs: v.n. To suffer injury, or damage, or loss
(-ko), To sustain an injury, to get hurt, &c.
(-ko), To stamp, coin; — to multiply (one number into, -meṅ, another)
v.t. To multiply ( = ẓarb denā, q.v.)
v.t. To strike, give (one) a blow; to hammer; — to stamp or impress (upon, -meṅ)
or ẓarb-ě-mas̤al, adj. & s.f. Constituted, or passed into, a proverb; proverbial; current; notorious; a form of saying, a common saying, a proverb, saw
or ẓarb-ḵẖāna, s.m. 'The stamping- or coining- house, the mint.

def: s.m. = P ضربت ẓarbat, s.f. (for A. ضربة, n. of un. fr. ẓarb, q.v.), One blow, a blow, a stroke, &c.
For this and other compounds beginning with zar which are not given below, see s.v. زر zar.
more: (زر Gold, and part. of بافتن To weave) s. Brocade, gold-tissue.

def: adj. Woven in gold, embroidered.
(زر Gold, and بفت from بافتن To weave) s. m. Brocade, cloth of gold.
s.f. = P ضربه ẓarba, s.m. (for A. ضربة, n. of un. fr. ẓarb, q.v.), One blow, a blow, a stroke, &c.
more: (noun of unity of ضرب) s. f. A blow.

def: s.f. pl. (of ẓarbat), Blows; strokes, &c.

ety: Pehl. zart; Zend zairit, rt. zar; S. हरित, rt. हृ
def: adj. Yellow (syn. pīlā); pale, pallid, wan, livid
more: adj. Yellow ( pīlā ); wan; livid. zard-ālū, zard-āṛū , n. m. An apricot. zard-āb , n. m. Serum. zard paṛnā yā ho jānā , v. n. To turn pale. zard pīlak , n. m. Oriolus kundu ; the golden oriole. It has a sweet song and comes in usually with the mango blossoms. It is known as the 'mango bird.' zard chob , n. f. lit . yellow wood. Turmeric; curcuma longa. zard rag, rag-i-jān , ( Anat .) Arteries. zard-rū , adj. Bashful; ashamed.

adj. Yellow, pale, wan, livid. زرد پيلک zard-pīlak, s. m. The name of a bird. زرد رنگ zard-rang, adj. 1. Yellow. 2. ( met. ) Bashful. زرد رو zard-rū, adj. Bashful, ashamed. زرد روٴي zard-rū-ī, s. f. Bashfulness. زرد گوش zard-gosh, adj. Hypocritical, malignant.
subs: s.m. 'Yellow water'; bile; serum; pus or matter running from a wound
s.m. The golden oriole, Oriolus kundu (commonly called the 'mango-bird,' because it arrives when the mango-tree blossoms)
adj. Yellow; (met.) bashful; s.m. The colour yellow
adj. & s.m. Hypocritical; malignant; — a hypocrite; a flatterer
v.n. To become pale or livid.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زرد (zard).

ety: fr. zard, q.v.
def: s.m. A sweet dish made of rice, raisins and almonds cooked in spices and coloured with saffron (a kind of pulāʼo, but without meat)
more: n. m. 1. Tobacco for chewing ( tambākū ); a quid. W. Maṅgvāyā zardah, chhālyā, tere ishq ne mujhe khā līyā . Now for tobacco, now for betel-nut she makes me run, This love for her is killing work, my life from me 's clean gone. 2. A kind of sweet pulāo , dressed with saffron. parde meṅ zardah lagānā , To commit adultery secretly. Woh to parde meṅ zardah lagātī haiṅ . Mah.
s. m. A kind of pulāʼo, dressed with saffron.
subs: (eaten with pān, or chewed alone; — cf. sūkhā); — the yolk of an egg (more commonly zardī, q.v.).

ety: zard, q.v.+aff. ak = S. क; or = zarda
def: adj. & s.f. Yellowish; — a carrot.
s. m. An apricot.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زردآلو (zard-ālū, literally “yellow plum”). Compare Gujarati જરદાળુ (jardāḷu), Kannada ಜರ್ದಾಳು (jardāḷu), Marathi जर्दाळू (jardāḷū), Punjabi ਜਰਦਾਲੂ (jardālū).

def: s. f. Yellow wood, turmerick. (Curcuma longa).

ety: zard, q.v.+Zend aff. ī = S. इ
def: s.f. Yellowness; yellow colour; paleness; jaundice; — yolk of an egg; — a gold mohur
more: n. f. 1. A yellow color; paleness. 2. The yolk of an egg. 3. The jaundice. 4. Yellow boys; a gold mohar. zardī chhānā , v. n. To turn pale.
s. f. 1. Yellowness. 2. The yolk of an egg.
subs: (-par), Paleness to come (over the face), to become pale.

ety: Pehl. zartusht; Zend zarathustra
def: prop. n. Zoroaster, founder of the religion of the Magi.
more: n. prop. Zoroaster, founder of the ancient religion of Persia.

ety: inf. n. of ظرف; see z̤arāfat
def: s.m. Ingenuity, skill, cleverness; beauty, excellence; elegance (of manners), politeness ( = z̤arāfat); — capacity, capability; a receptacle, vessel, vase; (in Gram.) an adverb (of time or place); — adj. Ingenious, skilful, clever; beautiful, graceful; elegant, polite
more: n. m. pl. zurūf , 1. A vessel ( bartan ). 2. Capacity; capability ( samāī ). 3. ( Gram .) An adverb of time and place. zarf-i-zamāṅ . Adverb of time. zarf-i-makāṅ . Adverb of place. ālī-zarf . Large-minded. kam-zarf, tunak-zarf . Shallow ( ochhā 1). zurūf-i-chīnī . China; porcelain. zurūf-i-nuqraī . Silver plates, spoons, etc.

s. m. 1. Ingenuity, elegance, politeness. 2. A vase, vessel; capability. 3. adj. Ingenious. 4. (in grammar) An adverb. ظرف زمان z̤arf-i-zamān, Adverb of time. ظرف مکان z̤arf-i-makān, Adverb of place. (Plur. ظروف z̤urūf, Vessels; adverbs, &c.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظَرْف (zarf), from Arabic ظَرْف (ẓarf).

ety: zar, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Anything woven with gold thread; gold lace; gold brocade
more: n. f. Any thing woven with gold thread. Zarī gotā purānā! Delhi Street cry. Any old gold lace to sell! zarī-bāf , n. m. A manufacturer in gold thread; a gold-lace worker. zar'rīṅ , adj. Golden ( sunahrī ).
(from زر) s. f. Any thing woven with gold thread. زري باف zarī-bāf, A gold-lace worker.
subs: s.m. A manufacture in gold thread; a gold-lace worker
s.f. The business or trade of gold-lace working

ety: zār, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Weeping, sighing, groaning, wailing, lamentation, begging and praying, supplication, entreaty, crying (or cry) for help.
more: n. f. Weeping and wailing; lamentation. Din kaṭā faryād se aur rāt zārī se kaṭī, Umr kaṭne ko kaṭī par kyā hi khuārī se kaṭī ! My days passed in complainings, and my nights in weepings vain, The measure of my days filled up, but filled, ah! with what pain!
s. f. Crying, sighing, groaning, lamentation, supplication, crying for help.

r: ẕarīʻa, ẕariʻa, ẕarīʻat, ẕirāʻ, orig, wasīla, ẕarīʻa paidā karnā, ẕarīʻa rakhnā, ẕarīʻe, ẕarīʻe-se, ba-ẕarīʻa, ke, is ẕarīʻe-se
def: (for A. ذريعة ẕarīʻat, fr. ذرع 'to stretch forth the arm'; cf. ẕirāʻ), s.m. orig. 'A cover or screen (as a beast, &c.) behind which a hunter lurks and approaches game'; (hence) a means of access or approach, medium, means, instrumentality, agency, cause, occasion, interest, favour, ingratiation, intervention, intercession (syn. wasīla)
more: n. m. ذرع stretching forth. 1. Medium; means, instrument; instrumentality; agency. 2. Influence; interest; favor. zariya paidā k. , v. n. To make interest. zariya rakhnā , v. n. To have interest. zariye se, ba-zariye , adv. In or by virtue of; through the intervention or instrumentality of; under cover; thereby; hereby. is zariye se , G. G. ( Law ) By these presents. ba-zariye tār-barqī , By telegram. ba-zariye ḍāk , By post. ba-zariye ohdeh-dārī, Ex-officio ; in virtue of one's office.
subs: To find means; to make interest
postpn., and ba-ẕarīʻa, prep., By means (of, -ke), by, by the medium (of), through the instrumentality or the intervention (of); in or by virtue (of); under cover (of)
By this means; hereby; by these presents.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذَرِیعَه (zarī'a), from Arabic ذَرِيعَة (ḏarīʕa, “pretext”).

ety: act. part. of ضرب 'to beat,' &c.
def: part. adj. Beating, bruising, striking; stamping, coining.
more: part. act. (of ضرب) Beating, bruising, striking, stamping, coining.

ety: v.n. fr. ظرف; see z̤arāfat
def: adj. & s.m. Clever, ingenious; elegant, polite, gallant; excellent, good; — witty, facetious, jocose, arch, comical, waggish; — a wit; a wag.
more: adj. Witty ( ṭhaṭol ). zarīf , n. m. A wit ( ṭhaṭol ).
(from ظرف) adj. 1. Ingenious, polite, gallant, good, elegant. 2. Witty, jocose, arch, comical, waggish.

ety: z̤arīf, q.v.+āna = Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. = z̤arīf; — adv. Ingeniously, skilfully, &c.; wittily, facetiously.

ety: prob. a corr. of A. ضريح ẓarīḥ
def: s.f. (local) A railing or lattice-work surrounding a temple or tomb.
more: s. f. A railing or lattice-work surrounding a temple or tomb.

ety: Zend zairina = S. हरिण
def: adj. Golden; made of gold; — zarīna, s.m. Golden stuffs or goods
more: (from زر) adj. Golden. زرين مرغ zarrīṅ murg̠ẖ, s. m. 1. The name of a bird. 2. ( met. ) The sun.
subs: s.m. Name of a bird: (met.) the sun.

ety: Zend zairina = S. हरिण
def: adj. Golden; made of gold; — zarīna, s.m. Golden stuffs or goods
more: (from زر) adj. Golden; golden stuffs or goods.
subs: s.m. Name of a bird: (met.) the sun.

ety: inf. n. of ضرط
def: s.m. Breaking wind.

ety: inf. n. of زرق 'to cast (the eye
def: s.m. Turning (the eye) towards an object (so that scarce anything but the white appears); — hypocrisy; fraud, imposture; — detraction
more: s. m. 1. Turning the eyes. 2. (in Pers.) Hypocrisy, fraud. 3. Detraction. زرق برق zarḳ-barḳ, s. m. Glare, glitter, show, splendid cloths, splendour.
subs: s.m. Glare, glitter, show, magnificence, pomp, splendour; glittering or shining cloth, splendid clothes; — thunder and lightning.

def: adj. See bhaṛak-dār , under bhaṛak , 1. Thunder-and-lightning; ( Slang ) gaudy. 2. Glittering; splendid.

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).
more: (or زرنه zarna or زرني zarnī ) s. m. Arsenick, orpiment. (Perhaps inverted from αρσενικον ).

def: s.m. = ẕarra, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), A little; — a little while, short time; a slight or trivial matter; — adv. Just, would you just, please, kindly (e.g. ẕarā yěh uṭhā-denā)
subs: adv. Little by little, by degrees; every bit, every jot and tittle
adj. (f. -ī -sī), A little; slight, trivial, trifling; — s.m. Just a little
adv. In a little while, in a very short time, soon, quickly; — with much trouble or difficulty.

ety: inf. n. of ضرّ 'to harm,' &c.
def: s.m. = ẓarar, q.v. s.m. Harm, injury, hurt, mischief, damage; defect, deficiency, detriment, loss; — poverty; affliction, distress, anguish
more: (Heb. Strait, adversity, hostility) s. m. Loss, damage, injury, distress.
subs: (-ko), To cause harm to come (to), to harm, hurt, &c.

ety: i.q. ẕarra, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), A little; — a little while, short time; a slight or trivial matter; — adv. Just, would you just, please, kindly (e.g. ẕarā yěh uṭhā-denā)
more: adv. A little ( thoṛā , 1). zarā , intj. Please! Do! Would you just! 1. Zarā yeh uṭhā denā! Would you take this up, please! 2. Na mujh se karo aise tum chochle, Na lipṭo, zarā haṭ-ke baiṭho pare! Wom. Shauq. zarā zarā, zarā zarā kar-ke , adv. Every bit; every jot and tittle; inch by inch. zarā sā, zarā sī , adj. A little. zarā kī zarā , adv. For an instant. zarā meṅ zarā , Little out of little. zarā men zarā chīz unheṅ bhī de detī hūṅ . Mah. Wom.
subs: adv. Little by little, by degrees; every bit, every jot and tittle
adj. (f. -ī -sī), A little; slight, trivial, trifling; — s.m. Just a little
adv. In a little while, in a very short time, soon, quickly; — with much trouble or difficulty.
ex: Close the door, if you may
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذره (zarra), from Arabic ذَرَّة (ḏarra).

ety: for A. ذرة ẕarrat, n. of un. fr. ذرّ 'to sprinkle, scatter,' &c.
def: s.m. A mote, atom, particle, a little, jot, tittle, an ace (see ẕarrā or ẕarā); — adj. (f. -ī), Little
more: n. m. A particle; an atom; a mote.
(from ذرّ) s. m. An atom, a particle, a little, a jot, a tittle, an ace. (Plur. ذرات). ذره وار ẕarra-wār, Like an atom, minute, a little.
s. m. A mote. adj. Little.
subs: ẕarra-wār, adj. Like an atom, minute, a little; humble, lowly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذره (zarra), from Arabic ذَرَّة (ḏarra).

def: (intens. n. fr. ضرب 'to beat,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'One who beats or strikes much'; a stamper (of coin), a coiner; a mint-master.
s.m. pl. (of ẕarra), Motes, atoms, particles, &c.

ety: dim. of ẕarra or ẕarrā, q.v.
def: s.f. A minute particle, an atom, a very little, &c.
more: (dimin. of ذره q. v. or fem.) An atom, a little, &c.

ety: v.n. fr. ضر 'to harm,' &c.
def: adj. & adv. Necessary, needful, requisite, expedient; urgent; unavoidable, indispensable, essential, imperative, &c.; — urgently; certainly, assuredly, of course; without fail; absolutely; peremptorily
more: adj. ضر necessitated. 1. Necessary; urgent; important. 2. Essential; indispensable. 3. Incumbent; obligatory; imperative. zurūr, zarūr ; Rus. jarūr , adv. 1. Urgently. 2. Certainly; of course; without fail; absolutely; peremptorily. zurūr zurūr, zurūr hī, bil-zurūr, zurūratan , adv. 1. Urgently; indispensably. 2. Ex necessitate rei ; necessarily; of or by necessity; perforce. zurūr hai . It is imperative; he is bound to. jāë-zurūr , A privy; necessary. jī zurūr , adv. ( Ironic .) 1. Certainly! of course! 2. Really! you dont say so!
(from ضرّ) adj. Necessary, expedient, unavoidable; sub. The necessary.
subs: or ẓarūr-hī, or biʼẓ-ẓarūr, adv. Necessarily, of necessity, perforce; urgently; indispensably
or jā-ẓarūr, s.m. A necessary, a privy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضرور (zurūr), from Arabic ضُرُور (ḍurūr).

ety: for A. ضرورة; see ẓarūr
def: s.f. Necessity, need, want, exigency; indigence; — compulsion, constraint, force, violence

Time of need. zurūrat-mand , Ped. adj. Necessitous ( hājatmand ). zurūrat meṅ kām ānā , v. n. To serve one in time of need. ba-qadr i-zurūrat . As needed. zurūrī chiṭṭhī , n. f. def: s.f. Possessor, owner, mistress (in these senses used as a prefixed noun; and often better rendered 'having, possessed of, endowed with'); essence, substance, nature, radical constituent; soul; body, person, self (i.e. a man's self, or a thing's self); generation, breed, tribe, caste; genus, species; sort, kind (in these senses, prob. connected, through the Persian, with the S. जाति; H. jāt; which the Hindūstānīs commonly corrupt into ẕāt); (in Gram.) a real substantive, a substantive ( = ism-ě-ẕāt, as opp. to ism-ě-maʻnī, and to ṣifat)
more: n. f. ذات essence. 1. State or form of being; nature; constitution; essence; substance. Yeh kapṛā zāt kā achchhā haī . 2. The body; person; self. Mujhe āp kī zāt kā baṛā bharosā hai . 3. (Cor. of H. jāt ) Origin ( asl ); birth; caste ( jāt , 2). 1. Ab to rupayā zāt hai . Prov. Money now is caste. 2. Inkī pāṅchvīṅ zāt hai . He is of the Fifth caste. (He has no caste). 3. Roṭī gaī muṅh meṅ, zāṭ gaī gū meṅ . Wom. Prov. The bread went down his throat, his caste into a filthy moat. (Said of an interested mesalliance or religious conversion). 4. Hāth beche haiṅ, kuchh zāt nahīṅ bechī . Prov. I've sold my limbs to you, not my caste. 5. Damṛī kī hāṅḍī ṭūṭī, kutte kī zāt pahchānī . Prov. A farthing pot broken, the dog known by this token. 4. Kind; sort; character; nature. Is zāt kā kapṛā lāo! zāt se girā-huā , An outcaste. zāt se nikālnā , v. a. To put out of caste. zāt-i-sharīf , ( Ironic .) A c…

(Sans. जाति ) The essence, nature, soul, self, substance, property, the body, person, cast, race, breed. ذات الجنب ẕātu-l-jamb, s. m. The pleurisy. ذات بهائي ẕāt-bhāʼī (dakh.) To deprive another of cast, by touching his food, &c. &c.

r: ze, zāʼe mǒhmala, zāʼe manqūt̤a, z., j, roj, roz, jor, zor, abjad
def: (called zāʼe mǒhmala or zāʼe manqūt̤a) is the sixteenth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the thirteenth of the Persian, and the eleventh of the Arabic), and has the sound of the English z. It has no corresponding letter in the Nāgarī alphabet, in which it is represented by ज़, or more commonly changed to ज j, as roj for roz, jor for zor, &c. In reckoning by abjad, it stands for 7; and in almanacs, it represents Saturday, and the sign Scorpio.
more: called zāë mo'jamā or zāë manqūta is the 11th letter of the Arabic, the 13th of the Persian, and the 6th of the Hindustānī alphabet. It has no corresponding letter in the Sanskrit alphabet, and ज़ is substituted to represent it in the Nāgrī character. Its sound is that of the English z. In the notation of numbers by the Arabic letters it stands for seven. In almanacs it represents Saturday, and the sign Scorpio. In Persian poetry it is used by way of contraction for az from, by, etc., as za-bas for az-bas , in abundance. It is interchangable as an initial, medial, and final with j , as P. zor =H. jor power; P. zamāna =H. jamāna time; P. tarzamān =P. tarjamān translator; P. kazāvah =P. kajāvah a camel's saddle; P. tezī =H. tejī sharpness; P. roz =P. roj day; P. arz =P. arj price; P. jahez =H. dahej dowry; P. āvāz =H. avāj sound. As a final it is interchangable with s , as Hurmuz = Hurmus ;…

is the eleventh letter of the Arabic, and the thirteenth of the Persian alphabet. It has no corresponding letter in the Sanskrit alphabet; and, ज ( j ) is substituted to represent it in the Nāgarī characters. Its sound is that of the English z. In the denotation of numbers by the Arabic letters, it stands for seven. In almanacks it represents Saturday, and the sign Scorpio. In poetry it is used by way of contraction for از From, by, &c. as زبس zi-bas, In abundance, although. It is changed (1) into ج as روج for روز. (2) into س as اياس for اياز A personʼs name. (3) into غ as گريغ for گريز. It is substituted (1) for چ as پزشک for پچشک A physician. (2) for ت in Arabic verbs of the form افتعال when ز is the first letter of the root; as, ازتان from زينت is changed to ازدان and that to ازّان.
